content.text: How can I check if a given object within an internal PE_PE library structure has been defined at the global level instead of inside a local scope?
If the PE_PE structure contained circular references, would this `is_global` function run infinitely?
Since there is no explicit mechanism to track visited nodes, yes; circular references in the hierarchy would cause infinite recursion and potentially crash the program or lead to a stack overflow.
Thanks for clarifying how the traversal works!
